subject: Computer Science
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: 3: INTRODUCTION TO PROGRAMMING
topics: This topic introduces learners to the fundamental concepts of programming through visual, block-based
environments. It focuses on how to think logically, sequence instructions, and create simple programs
activities: Classroom arranged for individual exploration and pair discussion
Clear view of board/projector for demonstrations and comparisons
Computers or laptops with internet access
Block-based programming software: Scratch, Blockly, MIT App Inventor, Code.org
Projector to show interface overview and examples
assessment: Scenario: In your Scratch class, a learner named Chileshe has created a simple animation of a goat (sprite).
He wants the goat to walk 10 steps, then bleat, then walk another 10 steps. He has put the blocks in this order:
play sound “Meee” → move 10 steps → move 10 steps.
(Debugging) When Chileshe runs his program, the goat bleats first, then moves 20 steps all at once.
What is wrong with his sequence? Explain how he should reorder the blocks to make it work as he
